  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:

      Convert a list of ``Triple`` objects into a fluent, grammatically correct sentence.
      All triples sharing the same subject are combined into a single clause;
      different subjects generate separate sentences.
      """

          return ""

with:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of ``Triple`` objects into a single fluent, grammatically correct sentence.
      All triples are combined into one complex sentence for better coherence.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      parts: list[str] = []
  
      for t in triples:
          s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object
  
          # Specific phrasing for common airport predicates
          if p == "cityServed":
              parts.append(f"{s} serves the city of {o}")
          elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel":
              parts.append(f"{s} is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level")
          elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet":
              parts.append(f"{s} is at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level")
          elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres":
              parts.append(f"{s} is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level")
          elif p == "location":
              parts.append(f"{s} is located in {o}")
          elif p == "operatingOrganisation":
              parts.append(f"{s} is operated by {o}")
          elif p == "runwayLength":
              parts.append(f"{s} has a runway length of {o} metres")
          elif p == "runwayName":
              parts.append(f"{s}'s runway is named {o}")
  ... (42 more lines)
